etymology.description: semantic 酉(酉) (yǒu, "alcohol") + phonetic abbreviation of 穌(穌) (sū, "revive") -> crisp; flaky
etymology.interpretation: The character 酥(sū, "crisp; flaky") combines the semantic part 酉(酉) (yǒu, "alcohol") to suggest a process or quality associated with alcohol, possibly invoking the idea of fermentation or texture, with the phonetic element 穌(穌) (sū, "revive") contributing the sound. The resulting character conveys a sense of something that is flaky or crisp, akin to the texture of certain pastries or substances made through fermentation.

examples.chinese: 这些酥饼非常美味(這些酥餅非常美味)。
examples.english: These flaky pastries are very delicious.

examples.chinese: 外面的炸鸡非常酥脆(外面的炸雞非常酥脆)。
examples.english: The fried chicken outside is very crispy.

examples.chinese: 他做的面包松软酥香(他做的麵包鬆軟酥香)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zuò de miànbāo sōngruǎn sū xiāng.
examples.english: The bread he made is soft and pleasantly flaky.
